summary: Read-only reviewer 02 RAG/retrieval quality review completed. Found plan directionally correct but not acceptable as written because semantic proof can be golden-query-bound while arbitrary live prompts still fall back to lexical retrieval.
decisions: User intent is a compounding LLM Wiki OS: reviewed session knowledge, source-backed claims, live pre-response context, behavior writeback, and policy-gated sync.
DISAGREE until completion criteria require dynamic live-query semantic retrieval, not only precomputed golden query vectors.
Generated-answer evaluation is currently local/extractive and should not count as full Ragas/LLM-judge quality evidence without calibration.
next_steps: Revise completion plan to add dynamic query embedding and bounded dense top-K vector search for arbitrary user prompts.
Update stale RAG blocker text that says semantic provider is not configured while current proof status shows HuggingFace embeddings.
Add hard tests for non-golden live prompts, paraphrase canaries, contribution metrics, latency, noise, and source-support budgets.
